headlines.title: Police investigate shooting that left man hurt in San Jose
headlines.description: San Jose police said they are investigating a shooting that sent a man to the hospital on Sunday. Police said they received a call just before noon regarding a shooting in the 2400 block of Amaryl Drive. At the scene, officers found a man with at least one gunshot wound, authorities said. Officials reported the man was taken to the hospital for non-life-threatening injuries. This is an ongoing investigation.

headlines.title: Bay Area congressman says armed Israeli settlers held him in West Bank
headlines.description: U.S. Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., says he was detained for more than an hour by armed Israeli settlers during a trip to the occupied West Bank, an encounter he said deepened his concerns about the conflict as he considers a 2028 presidential run. Khanna traveled to the region seeking an unfiltered look at life under Israeli occupation. Instead, he said he and his delegation were surrounded by settlers carrying American-made M4 rifles while visiting a destroyed Palestinian village. “We were just looking at it and these hoodlums come up with machine guns … and they detain us,” Khanna said. “They block off the road and then they call the IDF, and the IDF was on their side.” An aide traveling with the congressman said the group was held while members contacted the U.S. Embassy for help. The Israeli military said its troops responded after receiving reports that Israeli civilians were unlawfully blocking the road. The military said it dispersed the settlers and was not involved in detaining Khanna’s delegation. Israeli police said officers witnessed no violence, adding that the group had entered a closed military zone before being released. Khanna described the incident as an example of the “arrogance of power” operating with “total impunity.” “I saw the arrogance in the eyes of those settlers, 21- and 22-year-olds with guns, laughing that they had detained us,” Khanna said. “Having no respect that they were detaining Americans, no respect that there was an American congressperson in that bus.” The congressman later thanked a U.S. Embassy official for helping secure the group’s release and said he expects Israel to prosecute any violent settlers involved. The confrontation comes as the war in Gaza and conditions in the West Bank have emerged as sharp foreign policy divides within the Democratic Party. Khanna, who is seriously weighing a 2028 White House bid, said the experience has become a defining moment in how he views the region and his party’s future. “We need a new moral direction in our party, a new moral vision that respects the dignity and human rights of people around the world,” Khanna said. Guy Marzorati, a politics correspondent for KQED, noted that progressives like Khanna have used the conflict as a central issue in Democratic primaries across the country. “Khanna is someone who feels very comfortable talking about this issue of Israel, wanting to bring it front and center,” Marzorati said. “So clearly, looking ahead to 2028, this is something he’d want to bring front and center in a potential presidential campaign.” Israel has consistently rejected allegations of genocide in Gaza and apartheid in the West Bank, maintaining its military actions are necessary for national security.

headlines.title: Bay Area faces minor flooding as warm Pacific Ocean pushes tides higher
headlines.description: Waterways and low-lying areas around the Bay Area are under a coastal flood advisory through Thursday morning, as warming ocean temperatures and sea level rise threaten to push high tides well above normal levels. The National Weather Service issued the advisory for tidal waterways and low-lying spots around the San Francisco and San Pablo bays. High tides could reach up to 1.7 feet above average, potentially causing minor flooding on roads and in parks, including along San Francisco’s Embarcadero. Meteorologists attribute the unusually high water levels to a combination of sea level rise and thermal expansion caused by warmer-than-average temperatures across the Pacific Ocean. The rising waters are part of a recent pattern. Tides in the Bay Area reached 2 feet above normal in June and 2.5 feet above normal in January. At San Francisco’s Ocean Beach, visitors noted the unusually warm water but acknowledged the risks associated with the changing conditions. “I am very cautious of our water advisories, so I appreciate our science people,” said Karen Gehrman, who was visiting the beach. The San Francisco Fire Department is urging people near the water to remain vigilant while the advisory is in effect. Samuel Menchaca, a fire department spokesperson, reminded visitors about the dangers of sneaker waves, rip currents and the changing evolution of the ocean. “Never turn your back on the ocean, to be safe,” Menchaca said. Beachgoers familiar with the area know to keep an eye out for rip currents. “There are many of them and they change all the time, so it’s quite dangerous if you don’t know what you’re doing,” San Francisco resident Peter Cao said.

headlines.title: Authorities looking for missing woman last seen getting into Lyft vehicle in Oakland
headlines.description: The search is on for an at-risk missing woman last seen on Friday entering a Lyft vehicle near Highland Hospital in Oakland, according to the California Highway Patrol. CHP issued a Silver Alert on behalf of the Alameda County Sheriff’s Office for Brenda Hall. Hall was last seen on Friday, at around 2:20 p.m., in the 1400 block of E. 31st Street in Oakland. The sheriff’s office said it is believed Hall entered a Lyft vehicle with a possible destination of the 1200 block of Summerfield Road in Santa Rosa. The Silver Alert states the vehicle she might be in is a black 2023 Toyota Camry, California plate ED43V98. Authorities said Hall is 77 years old, 5 feet 6 inches tall, weighs around 130 pounds, and has short black and gray hair and brown eyes. The sheriff’s office said Hall was last seen wearing a pink beanie, a black and white stripped sweater and blue sweatpants. Anyone who sees Brenda Hall is encouraged to contact 911 or any local law enforcement agency.

headlines.title: Redwood City man charged in connection with 2 home invasion sexual assault cases
headlines.description: A 30-year-old Redwood City man has been charged in connection with two home invasion sexual assault cases in Redwood City and San Carlos after investigators from two law enforcement agencies linked him to both crimes. The Redwood City Police Department said officers responded July 5 to a home on Jackson Avenue, where investigators determined an intruder entered the residence while the victims were asleep and sexually assaulted an adult woman before fleeing. During the investigation, detectives identified Jose Manuel Gonzalez Cruz as a suspect and located him Tuesday in Redwood City. Authorities later determined he was also the suspect in a May 10 home invasion and sexual assault in San Carlos that was under investigation by the San Mateo County Sheriff’s Office. Cruz was booked into the Maguire Correctional Facility on Wednesday. The San Mateo County District Attorney’s Office filed charges Friday, including two counts of assault with intent to commit a sexual offense during a first-degree burglary, as well as attempted first-degree burglary, peeking or prying while loitering, and indecent exposure. Authorities asked anyone with information about either investigation or who believes they had similar contact with Cruz to contact the Redwood City Police Department at (650) 780-7100 or the San Mateo County Sheriff’s Office at 650-363-4911.

headlines.title: Mechanical issue halts 2 San Francisco cable car lines
headlines.description: The Powell-Hyde and California cable car lines will be out of service for the remainder of the day due to a mechanical issue, transit officials said Sunday. Bus shuttles are operating to support the affected routes. The Powell-Mason cable car line is continuing normal service.

headlines.title: SF shooting leaves man with serious injuries, suspect at large
headlines.description: A man was hospitalized with life-threatening injuries following a shooting early Sunday in San Francisco, police said. Officers responded to a report of a shooting near Fern Street and Van Ness Avenue at about 1:11 a.m. They found a man suffering from a gunshot wound and provided medical aid until paramedics arrived, according to the San Francisco Police Department. The victim was transported to a local hospital. No arrests have been made, and the investigation remains active. Police are asking anyone with information to call the SFPD tip line at 415-575-4444 or text a tip to TIP411 with “SFPD” at the start of the message.

headlines.title: Hang glider taken to the hospital after crash along San Francisco coast
headlines.description: A hang glider was taken to the hospital following a crash along the San Francisco coast on Saturday, according to the fire department. Fire crews say the person was rescued from the beach near the entrance to Fort Funston. They say the hang glider was taken to a trauma center in an ambulance.

headlines.title: Woman found dead at suspected illicit cannabis farm in Mendocino County
headlines.description: A 31-year-old Covelo woman was found dead on Thursday after apparently being shot multiple times at a suspected illicit cannabis farm in Mendocino County. The woman’s body was found in a pickup truck in the 22000 block of Rifle Range Road, in an unincorporated area northwest of Covelo, the Mendocino County Sheriff’s Office said on Saturday. Sheriff’s deputies were called to the scene by a family member of the unidentified victim, who told deputies she and other relatives had been looking for the victim after she went missing that day. The relative told deputies she went to the alleged cannabis farm to try to find the victim after suspecting she might be there and found her at about 9 p.m. in a silver 2007 Toyota Tacoma pickup truck. The victim had been shot multiple times and was declared dead after sheriff’s deputies and investigators from the Mendocino County District Attorney’s Office and state Department of Justice arrived on scene. The Sheriff’s Office said in a statement that there were “numerous suspects” and that the involvement of the cannabis farm and its operators were being investigated. Anyone with information about the shooting was asked to contact the Mendocino County Sheriff’s Office at 707-463-4086.

headlines.title: San Francisco hosts its first ever ‘One City Day'
headlines.description: Thousands of people participated in “One City Day,” a community event where volunteers are invited to serve San Francisco. Multiple service projects were hosted in over 200 locations in the city, where approximately 3,500 people showed up to give back to the community. A volunteer says the area looked pretty clean already, but it made sense to help. Service activities ranged from picking up trash from the streets to making toys for shelter animals. Mayor Daniel Lurie said that his wife, Becca, came up with the idea to start One City Day because of her commitment to volunteer work. This event marked the first ever One City Day and has already made an impact. “This is just a start for so many people but its also a continuation for other groups that are doing this day in and day out and now we have a new group of San Franciscans coming to support our cities rise,” Mayor Lurie said.

headlines.title: Napa man accused of firing a handgun at a South San Francisco hotel
headlines.description: A Napa man was arrested after reports of gunfire at the Park Pointe Hotel in June, the South San Francisco police said on Saturday. Police said they received multiple calls of shots being fired at around 3:40 a.m. on June 28 at the hotel parking lot on 245 S. Airport Blvd., and arrived on scene within about two minutes. Authorities said the suspect, identified as Jose Basultollamas, was accused of firing a handgun into the air. Officials reported the suspect was located from witnesses and drone support. Police said the man briefly ran away and attempted to hide on the second floor of the hotel before being taken into custody without incident. Police said they recovered the loaded firearm, and no injuries were reported. Basultollamas is scheduled for a preliminary hearing on July 14 with his bail at $25,000, according to the police department.

headlines.title: Democratic congressman says he was detained by the Israeli military and settlers in the West Bank
headlines.description: U.S. Rep. Ro Khanna said Saturday that he was detained by settlers and the Israeli military in the occupied West Bank and released only after calls to the American Embassy in Jerusalem. The Israeli Defense Forces denied detaining any visitors in the incident, the latest example of escalating political tensions involving Israel and its ally’s Democratic Party. A representative for Khanna, a California Democrat who is an outspoken progressive, said the confrontation occurred Wednesday in the middle of a three-day tour of the West Bank. As the congressman visited a Palestinian village that had been abandoned after settler attacks, masked men with guns stopped his group and refused to let them leave. The New York Times said the incident was witnessed by one of its photographers. Khanna’s office said it occurred in the town of Khirbet Zanuta. Khanna said that when Israeli soldiers arrived he was dispirited to see them interact in a friendly manner with their captors and also block the exit for the congressman’s party. Not until the U.S. Embassy and Israeli police were called was Khanna’s group allowed to proceed. “If this can happen to an American member of Congress, imagine what life is like for Palestinians who have no smartphones, no security, and no national platform,” Khanna, who is exploring a presidential bid in 2028, said in a fundraising email he sent out shortly after his post Saturday about the incident. terrorism Jun 30 War with Iran Jun 21 In a statement, the IDF said it received a report of Israeli citizens blocking foreign nationals and media in Khirbet Zanuta. “Upon receiving the report, IDF troops were dispatched to the scene, quickly dispersed the Israeli civilians, and reopened the blocked road,” the military said in a statement. “The IDF soldiers operating in the area did not take part in blocking the road.” Democratic politicians from the United States have stepped up their criticism of Israel amid a sharp turn against the country by the party’s voters. This week, former Chicago Mayor Rahm Emanuel, whose father was born in Jerusalem and fought in Israel’s war of independence, gave a blistering speech last week in Tel Aviv in which he said Israel has become a “territorial pariah.” Emanuel also is a potential White House contender. In a recent survey by The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research, about 58% of Democrats said the U.S. is “too supportive” of Israelis. Also Saturday, the Israeli military said it detained four suspects who were attacking foreign journalists traveling to Sinjil, another West Bank community. The assailants blocked the journalists’ vehicle and damaged it and were armed with clubs and knives, according to the military’s statement. CNN reported that it had a team among the journalists who were attacked. The network said the journalists were there to cover the one-year anniversary of the killing of a Palestinian-American man who was beaten to death by Israeli settlers.
